# Route Planning

> Plan multi-stop delivery routes with ordered waypoints, route optimization, and distance/time estimation

**Category:** Workflow · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** fleet · route · waypoints · stops · navigation · optimization

## What this does

Plan multi-stop delivery routes with ordered waypoints, route optimization, and distance/time estimation

Specifies 5 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **route_uuid** *(text, required)* — Route ID
- **order_uuid** *(text, required)* — Order
- **payload_uuid** *(text, required)* — Payload
- **pickup_uuid** *(text, required)* — Pickup Location
- **dropoff_uuid** *(text, required)* — Dropoff Location
- **return_uuid** *(text, optional)* — Return Location
- **current_waypoint_uuid** *(text, optional)* — Current Waypoint
- **waypoints** *(json, optional)* — Waypoints
- **total_distance** *(number, optional)* — Total Distance (m)
- **total_time** *(number, optional)* — Total Time (s)
- **is_route_optimized** *(boolean, optional)* — Route Optimized
- **route_details** *(json, optional)* — Turn-by-Turn Details
- **status** *(select, required)* — Status

## What must be true

- **minimum_waypoints:** A route must have at least one pickup and one dropoff location
- **sequential_waypoints:** Waypoints are ordered; the driver must visit them in sequence
- **optimization_preserves_endpoints:** Route optimization reorders intermediate stops while preserving first pickup and last dropoff
- **geospatial_calculation:** Distance and time estimates are calculated using a geospatial routing service
- **waypoint_advancement:** When a waypoint is completed, current_waypoint_uuid advances to the next stop
- **per_waypoint_tracking:** Each waypoint has its own tracking number for independent status updates
- **dispatcher_approval_for_changes:** Adding or removing waypoints after dispatch requires dispatcher approval
- **round_trip_support:** Round-trip routes include a return waypoint equal to the origin
- **audit_route_details:** Route details including turn-by-turn data are stored for auditing and replay
- **valid_coordinates:** Each place reference must have valid latitude and longitude coordinates

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Route Created** — when pickup_uuid exists; dropoff_uuid exists, then Route created with calculated distance and time.
- **Route Optimized** — when route has 3 or more waypoints; optimization has not been applied, then Waypoint order optimized to minimize travel distance.
- **Waypoint Completed** — when driver confirms arrival at current waypoint, then Current waypoint marked complete; driver advances to next stop.
- **Route Completed** — when all waypoints are completed, then All stops visited; route is complete.

**❌ Failure paths**

- **Missing Coordinates** — when a place reference has no valid coordinates, then Route creation failed — location coordinates missing. *(error: `ROUTE_MISSING_COORDINATES`)*

## Errors it can return

- `ROUTE_MISSING_COORDINATES` — One or more locations are missing valid coordinates.
- `ROUTE_CALCULATION_FAILED` — Route could not be calculated. Please check all locations and try again.
- `ROUTE_INVALID_WAYPOINT_ORDER` — Waypoint sequence is invalid.

## Connects to

- **order-lifecycle** *(required)* — Routes are created for and attached to orders
- **realtime-driver-tracking** *(recommended)* — Driver position is tracked against route progress
- **order-sla-eta** *(recommended)* — Route time estimates feed into ETA calculations
- **proof-of-delivery** *(optional)* — POD collected at final delivery waypoint
